data.title: Isomorphic Rust, route-split WASM
data.excerpt: Same code on the server and the browser, with route-level WASM chunks shipped under brotli.
data.body:
# Isomorphic Rust, route-split WASM

Ferro's admin UI is a Leptos app that runs on the server (SSR shell) **and** the browser (CSR mount). Same component tree, same types, same compiler errors when you typo a field name.

## How the bundle stays small

- `cargo leptos build --split` carves the WASM into per-route chunks.
- `wasm-opt` trims dead code.
- The build pipeline writes brotli-precompressed `.br` siblings.
- `tower-http`'s `ServeDir::precompressed_br()` honors `Accept-Encoding` and serves the `.br` straight to the browser.

Result: a small initial payload, with subsequent routes pulled lazily.

## What you give up

You take a hit on cold compile time. The first build of the WASM lib is several minutes; incremental rebuilds are seconds. For most teams that trade compiles for type-safety on every line of UI code, the math works.

## What you get

- Field editors share types with the server's storage layer. Renaming a field is one PR.
- Server functions are typed end-to-end. No JSON shape mismatches.
- Memory safety extends to the editor — XSS via mistakenly rendered raw HTML is a compile error.
